title: C Header Files
content:
### Header Files in C

Header files (`.h` extension) contain declarations shared across multiple `.c` files.

### What Goes in Headers

- Function prototypes
- Macro definitions (`#define`)
- Type definitions (`typedef`, `struct`, `union`, `enum`)
- Global variable declarations (`extern`)
- Constants

### What Does NOT Go in Headers

- Function definitions (except inline)
- Variable definitions

### Standard Header Files

| Header | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `<stdio.h>` | Input/output functions |
| `<stdlib.h>` | Memory allocation, conversions |
| `<string.h>` | String manipulation |
| `<math.h>` | Mathematical functions |
| `<ctype.h>` | Character handling |
| `<time.h>` | Date and time |
| `<stdbool.h>` | Boolean type |
| `<stdint.h>` | Fixed-width integer types |
| `<errno.h>` | Error codes |
| `<assert.h>` | Debug assertions |
| `<signal.h>` | Signal handling |
| `<setjmp.h>` | Non-local jumps |

### Include Guards

Prevent multiple inclusions of the same header:

```c
#ifndef MY_HEADER_H
#define MY_HEADER_H
// declarations
#endif
```

Or use `#pragma once` (non-standard but widely supported).

---

> **Key Point:** Use include guards to prevent multiple inclusions. Header files should declare but not define (with some exceptions like inline functions).

quiz.question: What is the purpose of include guards?
